questions.question: Explain the difference between image processing and computer vision with examples.
keyPoints: Image processing transforms pixel values (filtering, enhancement)
Computer vision interprets content to understand scene
Examples: Image processing - filters, compression; Computer vision - object detection, face recognition
Computer vision builds on image processing results

questions.question: Describe the steps involved in the Canny edge detection algorithm.
keyPoints: Gaussian smoothing to reduce noise
Gradient calculation using Sobel operators
Non-maximum suppression to thin edges
Double thresholding to classify edges
Edge tracking by hysteresis to connect strong edges
Final output produces thin, connected edges

questions.question: What is morphological opening? Explain its applications in image processing.
keyPoints: Opening = erosion followed by dilation
Removes small bright objects and smooths boundaries
Preserves larger shapes and objects
Applications: noise removal, breaking narrow connections, smoothing contours
Useful in preprocessing for object detection

questions.question: Explain chain codes for boundary representation. How are 4-connectivity and 8-connectivity chain codes different?
keyPoints: Chain codes encode boundary as sequence of direction codes
4-connectivity uses 4 directions (0-3): right, down, left, up
8-connectivity uses 8 directions (0-7): all compass directions
Difference in diagonal movement allowed and boundary smoothness
Used for boundary description, shape analysis, object recognition

questions.question: What is the Hit-or-Miss transform in morphological image processing?
keyPoints: Used for pattern detection in binary images
Detects locations where structuring element matches exactly
Requires both foreground and background patterns
Applications: shape detection, character recognition, object localization
Uses two structuring elements for foreground and background

questions.question: Discuss template matching technique in computer vision with its limitations.
keyPoints: Template matching slides template over image to find matches
Computes similarity measures: SSD, NCC, correlation
Limitations: sensitive to rotation, scale changes, occlusion, illumination variations
Computationally expensive for large images
Works best when object appearance is consistent

questions.question: Explain the concept of image segmentation and mention any two methods used for segmentation.
keyPoints: Segmentation partitions image into meaningful regions
Methods: thresholding, region growing, clustering, watershed, active contours
Thresholding: separates based on intensity values
Region growing: expands from seed pixels based on similarity
Goal: simplify representation for easier analysis

questions.question: Explain the boundary following algorithm (Moore-Neighbor tracing) in detail. How are chain codes generated from this process?
keyPoints: Start from boundary pixel and trace entire boundary
Use 8-connectivity to find neighboring boundary pixels
Algorithm: 1) Find starting boundary pixel 2) Check neighbors in specific order 3) Move to first found boundary pixel 4) Repeat until returning to start
Chain codes generated by recording direction of each move
Direction codes 0-7 for 8-connectivity represent movement directions
Resulting sequence represents complete boundary shape
Applications: shape analysis, perimeter calculation, object recognition

questions.question: Describe the fundamental concepts of computer vision. Discuss its applications in various domains.
keyPoints: Computer vision enables machines to interpret visual information
Key concepts: image acquisition, preprocessing, feature extraction, segmentation, recognition
Applications: Healthcare (medical imaging), Automotive (autonomous vehicles), Security (face detection), Retail (product recognition), Agriculture (crop monitoring), Manufacturing (quality control)
Advances in deep learning have significantly improved accuracy
Interdisciplinary field combining AI, image processing, pattern recognition

questions.question: Explain morphological image processing operations: erosion, dilation, opening, and closing. Discuss their properties and applications.
keyPoints: Erosion: shrinks objects, removes boundary pixels, uses structuring element
Dilation: expands objects, fills holes, adds boundary pixels
Opening: erosion followed by dilation, removes small objects, smooths boundaries
Closing: dilation followed by erosion, fills small holes, connects objects
Properties: idempotent operations, duality between erosion and dilation
Applications: noise removal, object extraction, shape analysis, boundary extraction, image enhancement
Choice of structuring element affects results significantly

questions.question: Discuss feature extraction techniques in computer vision. What are the key features used for image analysis?
keyPoints: Feature extraction computes distinctive image characteristics
Key features: edges, corners, blobs, textures, shapes, colors
Edge detection: Canny, Sobel, Prewitt operators
Corner detection: Harris, FAST algorithm
Texture features: GLCM, LBP, Gabor filters
Color features: histograms, color moments, color coherence vectors
Features should be distinctive, repeatable, and computable efficiently
Feature vectors used for classification, recognition, matching
